questions.stem: —______ you ______ your homework yet? —Yes, I ______ it an hour ago.
questions.explanation: 解析：第一空根据关键词yet（常用于现在完成时疑问句和否定句）可知用现在完成时Have...finished；第二空根据ago（一般过去时标志词）可知用finished。A项第一空错误，yet不与一般过去时连用；C项第二空错误，ago提示用过去时而非完成时；D项两空均错误。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is standing under the tree is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 解析：定语从句先行词the boy指人，排除B项which（指物）；从句中缺少主语，应用主格who。C项whom是宾格，在从句中作宾语时使用；D项whose表示'……的'，在从句中作定语。口诀：先行词指人作主语，who来引导莫犹豫。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the library tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 解析：tomorrow提示用一般将来时；主语these books是动词return的承受者，应用被动语态。一般将来时被动语态结构为will be+过去分词。A项是主动语态；C项是现在进行时主动；D项是一般过去时。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: My father ______ the club for five years.
questions.explanation: 解析：for five years是时间段，要求谓语动词用延续性动词。join是短暂性动词，不能与时间段连用，需转换为be in或be a member of。A项has joined错误，短暂动词不能接时间段；C项joined不能与for five years连用；D项一般现在时表示习惯，与语境不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It's ______ to eat too much junk food. It's bad for our health.
questions.explanation: 解析：be动词后接形容词作表语，排除C（名词）和D（副词）；根据后句'对身体有害'可知选unhealthy（不健康的）。A项healthy（健康的）与句意相反。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 解析：im-是否定前缀，加在possible（可能的）前构成impossible（不可能的）。A项polite（礼貌的）否定形式是impolite；C项patient（耐心的）否定形式是impatient；D项perfect（完美的）否定形式是imperfect。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We need to ______ a solution to the pollution problem.
questions.explanation: 解析：come up with意为'提出，想出（主意、方案）'；come out意为'出版，发行，开花'；come on意为'加油，快点'；come true意为'实现'。根据宾语a solution（解决方案）可知选A。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green program last month. The program aims to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and waste paper.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______ to carry the heavy bags to the collection point. However, after learning that these materials could be (9)______ into new products, they became more interested. "It's amazing to see how waste can be turned into something useful," said Li Ming, a student from Class 3.

The school also set up a small garden where students could grow vegetables. This activity not only teaches students about nature but also helps them relax from their busy (10)______. "I feel proud when I see the plants growing," said one student.

So far, the program has been very (11)______. The amount of waste produced by the school has dropped by 30%. The headmaster said they would (12)______ the program next semester because of its great success.
questions.explanation: 解析：根据上下文，刚开始学生觉得把沉重的袋子搬到收集点很'困难'（difficult）。后文的However表示转折，说明后来态度改变。A项easy（容易的）与后文态度转变逻辑不符；C项interesting（有趣的）与at first的语境不符；D项necessary（必要的）不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green program last month. The program aims to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and waste paper.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______ to carry the heavy bags to the collection point. However, after learning that these materials could be (9)______ into new products, they became more interested. "It's amazing to see how waste can be turned into something useful," said Li Ming, a student from Class 3.

The school also set up a small garden where students could grow vegetables. This activity not only teaches students about nature but also helps them relax from their busy (10)______. "I feel proud when I see the plants growing," said one student.

So far, the program has been very (11)______. The amount of waste produced by the school has dropped by 30%. The headmaster said they would (12)______ the program next semester because of its great success.
questions.explanation: 解析：turn...into...是固定搭配，意为'把……变成……'，被动形式为be turned into。后文也出现turned into的原句复现。A项made需与into搭配make into（把……制成），但通常指彻底改变性质；B项divided into（被分成）；D项cut into（被切成）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green program last month. The program aims to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and waste paper.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______ to carry the heavy bags to the collection point. However, after learning that these materials could be (9)______ into new products, they became more interested. "It's amazing to see how waste can be turned into something useful," said Li Ming, a student from Class 3.

The school also set up a small garden where students could grow vegetables. This activity not only teaches students about nature but also helps them relax from their busy (10)______. "I feel proud when I see the plants growing," said one student.

So far, the program has been very (11)______. The amount of waste produced by the school has dropped by 30%. The headmaster said they would (12)______ the program next semester because of its great success.
questions.explanation: 解析：schedule（日程安排，时间表）与busy搭配最恰当，指从繁忙的日程中放松。A项study通常不与busy直接搭配；C项homework虽然可以说busy homework，但不如schedule涵盖面广；D项housework（家务）与学校语境不符。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green program last month. The program aims to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and waste paper.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______ to carry the heavy bags to the collection point. However, after learning that these materials could be (9)______ into new products, they became more interested. "It's amazing to see how waste can be turned into something useful," said Li Ming, a student from Class 3.

The school also set up a small garden where students could grow vegetables. This activity not only teaches students about nature but also helps them relax from their busy (10)______. "I feel proud when I see the plants growing," said one student.

So far, the program has been very (11)______. The amount of waste produced by the school has dropped by 30%. The headmaster said they would (12)______ the program next semester because of its great success.
questions.explanation: 解析：根据后文'垃圾量减少30%'和'because of its great success'可知项目很'成功'（successful）。A项difficult（困难的）与后文积极描述矛盾；B项different（不同的）不符合语境；D项harmful（有害的）与文意相反。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Our school started a green program last month. The program aims to help students understand the importance of protecting the environment. Every class was asked to collect recyclable materials like plastic bottles and waste paper.

At first, many students thought it was (8)______ to carry the heavy bags to the collection point. However, after learning that these materials could be (9)______ into new products, they became more interested. "It's amazing to see how waste can be turned into something useful," said Li Ming, a student from Class 3.

The school also set up a small garden where students could grow vegetables. This activity not only teaches students about nature but also helps them relax from their busy (10)______. "I feel proud when I see the plants growing," said one student.

So far, the program has been very (11)______. The amount of waste produced by the school has dropped by 30%. The headmaster said they would (12)______ the program next semester because of its great success.
questions.explanation: 解析：根据'because of its great success'（因为很成功）可知下学期会'继续'（continue）这个项目。A项stop（停止）与文意相反；C项change（改变）没有依据；D项forget（忘记）不符合逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Mobile payment has changed the way we live in China. With just a smartphone, people can buy almost everything without carrying cash or cards.

The technology behind mobile payment is called QR code (二维码). When you want to pay, you simply open your payment app and let the seller scan your code, or you scan the seller's code. The money moves from your account to the seller's in seconds.

However, this convenience also brings concerns. Some people worry about the safety of their personal information. If someone steals your phone, they might access your money. To solve this problem, payment companies have developed fingerprint and face recognition technologies. These features ensure that only the owner can use the payment app.

Experts believe that mobile payment will become even more popular in the future. It not only saves time but also reduces the cost of handling cash for businesses. As one expert said, "We are moving towards a cashless society, and this is just the beginning."
questions.stem: How does mobile payment work according to the passage?
questions.explanation: 解析：细节理解题。根据第二段'TThe technology behind mobile payment is called QR code'以及'let the seller scan your code, or you scan the seller's code'可知，移动支付通过扫描二维码完成。A项使用现金与文中without carrying cash矛盾；C项打电话给银行、D项发短信均未提及。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Mobile payment has changed the way we live in China. With just a smartphone, people can buy almost everything without carrying cash or cards.

The technology behind mobile payment is called QR code (二维码). When you want to pay, you simply open your payment app and let the seller scan your code, or you scan the seller's code. The money moves from your account to the seller's in seconds.

However, this convenience also brings concerns. Some people worry about the safety of their personal information. If someone steals your phone, they might access your money. To solve this problem, payment companies have developed fingerprint and face recognition technologies. These features ensure that only the owner can use the payment app.

Experts believe that mobile payment will become even more popular in the future. It not only saves time but also reduces the cost of handling cash for businesses. As one expert said, "We are moving towards a cashless society, and this is just the beginning."
questions.stem: What does the word 'concerns' in Paragraph 3 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 解析：词义猜测题。根据后文'Some people worry about the safety...'（有些人担心安全……）可知concerns与worries同义，意为'担忧'。B项advantages（优点）、C项instructions（说明）、D项results（结果）均不符合语境。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Mobile payment has changed the way we live in China. With just a smartphone, people can buy almost everything without carrying cash or cards.

The technology behind mobile payment is called QR code (二维码). When you want to pay, you simply open your payment app and let the seller scan your code, or you scan the seller's code. The money moves from your account to the seller's in seconds.

However, this convenience also brings concerns. Some people worry about the safety of their personal information. If someone steals your phone, they might access your money. To solve this problem, payment companies have developed fingerprint and face recognition technologies. These features ensure that only the owner can use the payment app.

Experts believe that mobile payment will become even more popular in the future. It not only saves time but also reduces the cost of handling cash for businesses. As one expert said, "We are moving towards a cashless society, and this is just the beginning."
questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage?
options: A. Mobile payment is only popular in big cities.
B. People don't like using cash anymore.
C. Fingerprint technology makes mobile payment safer.
D. QR codes were invented by payment companies.
questions.explanation: 解析：推理判断题。根据第三段'TTo solve this problem, payment companies have developed fingerprint and face recognition technologies. These features ensure that only the owner can use the payment app'可推断，指纹技术使移动支付更安全。A项only popular in big cities（只在大城市流行）文中未提；B项don't like using cash（不喜欢用现金）过于绝对；D项QR codes were invented by payment companies（二维码由支付公司发明）文中未提，且与事实不符。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Mobile payment has changed the way we live in China. With just a smartphone, people can buy almost everything without carrying cash or cards.

The technology behind mobile payment is called QR code (二维码). When you want to pay, you simply open your payment app and let the seller scan your code, or you scan the seller's code. The money moves from your account to the seller's in seconds.

However, this convenience also brings concerns. Some people worry about the safety of their personal information. If someone steals your phone, they might access your money. To solve this problem, payment companies have developed fingerprint and face recognition technologies. These features ensure that only the owner can use the payment app.

Experts believe that mobile payment will become even more popular in the future. It not only saves time but also reduces the cost of handling cash for businesses. As one expert said, "We are moving towards a cashless society, and this is just the beginning."
questions.stem: What is the best title for the passage?
options: C. Mobile Payment: Changing Our Lives
questions.explanation: 解析：主旨大意题。文章首句'MMobile payment has changed the way we live'点明主题，全文围绕移动支付如何改变生活、其工作原理、安全问题及未来发展展开。A项货币历史、B项如何使用手机、D项科技的危险均偏离主题。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you achieve a balance between study and relaxation.

First, make a proper plan for your time. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next. Write down your tasks and finish them one by one.

Second, don't forget to exercise. Physical activity can help reduce stress and improve your concentration. (19)______ Even a 20-minute walk can make a big difference.

(20)______ If you spend all your time studying, you will feel tired and bored. Taking breaks helps your brain work better.
options: A. However, it's important to balance study and rest.
B. For example, you can play basketball or go jogging after school.
C. In addition, remember to take short breaks during long study periods.
D. Then, you should focus on your homework without distractions.
E. Know exactly what you need to do each hour.
questions.explanation: 解析：前文提到学业压力，后文给出平衡学习与放松的建议，此处需要转折过渡。A项'然而，平衡学习与休息很重要'承上启下。B项举例运动；C项补充休息；D项讲专注；E项讲具体计划，均不符合此处逻辑。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you achieve a balance between study and relaxation.

First, make a proper plan for your time. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next. Write down your tasks and finish them one by one.

Second, don't forget to exercise. Physical activity can help reduce stress and improve your concentration. (19)______ Even a 20-minute walk can make a big difference.

(20)______ If you spend all your time studying, you will feel tired and bored. Taking breaks helps your brain work better.
options: A. However, it's important to balance study and rest.
B. For example, you can play basketball or go jogging after school.
C. In addition, remember to take short breaks during long study periods.
D. Then, you should focus on your homework without distractions.
E. Know exactly what you need to do each hour.
questions.explanation: 解析：前句说'制定合适的时间计划'，E项'清楚知道每小时要做什么'是对计划的具体解释，与后文'这样就不会浪费时间决定下一步做什么'逻辑连贯。A项是总起句；B项讲运动；C项讲休息；D项讲专注，均不符。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you achieve a balance between study and relaxation.

First, make a proper plan for your time. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next. Write down your tasks and finish them one by one.

Second, don't forget to exercise. Physical activity can help reduce stress and improve your concentration. (19)______ Even a 20-minute walk can make a big difference.

(20)______ If you spend all your time studying, you will feel tired and bored. Taking breaks helps your brain work better.
options: A. However, it's important to balance study and rest.
B. For example, you can play basketball or go jogging after school.
C. In addition, remember to take short breaks during long study periods.
D. Then, you should focus on your homework without distractions.
E. Know exactly what you need to do each hour.
questions.explanation: 解析：前句讲'体育活动有助于减压'，后句讲'即使20分钟步行也有很大作用'，B项'例如，你可以放学后打篮球或慢跑'举例说明运动方式，承上启下。A项是总起；C项讲休息；D项讲专注；E项讲计划，均不符。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Many middle school students feel stressed because of heavy schoolwork. (17)______ Here are some useful tips to help you achieve a balance between study and relaxation.

First, make a proper plan for your time. (18)______ This way, you won't waste time deciding what to do next. Write down your tasks and finish them one by one.

Second, don't forget to exercise. Physical activity can help reduce stress and improve your concentration. (19)______ Even a 20-minute walk can make a big difference.

(20)______ If you spend all your time studying, you will feel tired and bored. Taking breaks helps your brain work better.
options: A. However, it's important to balance study and rest.
B. For example, you can play basketball or go jogging after school.
C. In addition, remember to take short breaks during long study periods.
D. Then, you should focus on your homework without distractions.
E. Know exactly what you need to do each hour.
questions.explanation: 解析：后文讲'如果一直学习会感到疲惫，休息帮助大脑更好工作'，C项'此外，记得在长时间学习期间短暂休息'引出该话题。A项已用于17题；B项讲运动；D项讲专注；E项讲计划，均不符。
questions.label: 短文填空
